reason: The remediation only changes the permissions of the backup shadow file (/etc/shadow-). This does not affect the active authentication database (/etc/shadow) and therefore poses no risk of locking out users or breaking services. The operation is a standard file permission hardening step.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential typo could target the live /etc/shadow instead of the backup; Incorrect chmod syntax could leave the file world‑readable
